name: Soba in Chicken Broth
recipeIngredient: 1/4 pound Fresh shiitake mushrooms, stems removed, slivered (about 1 cup)
2 tablespoons Tamari or reduced-sodium soy sauce
1 cup Packed spinach leaves, washed, dried and coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, bring at least 3 quarts of water to a boil.
2. Slowly add the soba noodles. When the water returns to a boil, add 1/2 cup cold water. Repeat the process of returning the water to a boil and adding cold water 2 or 3 times until the noodles are just tender (it will take 5 to 7 minutes total).
3. Drain and rinse well, working your fingers through the strands to separate them. Set aside.
4. Combine the chicken broth and ginger in the large pot and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to medium-low, add mushrooms, and simmer for 8 minutes.
5. Add sake or mirin, tamari or soy sauce, and rice-wine vinegar. In a small bowl, whisk a ladleful of the broth with miso to dissolve it; return the mixture to the pot, along with spinach. Simmer for 2 more minutes and remove from the heat.
6. Divide the noodles among soup bowls and ladle the soup over the top. Garnish with scallions and daikon.
